title: Powell’s Chair Ends but He Keeps His Board Seat
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Jerome Powell's term as Fed Chair ended in May 2024, but he retained his position as a member of the Board of Governors. Powell's decision to stay on the board
excerpt: Jerome Powell's term as Fed Chair ended in May 2024, but he retained his position as a member of the Board of Governors. Powell's decision to stay on the board prevents President Trump from quickly nominating his replacement. Powell's term as a governor runs until 2028.

content.introduction: Jerome Powell's term as Fed Chair ended in May 2024, but he retained his position as a member of the Board of Governors. Powell's decision to stay on the board prevents President Trump from quickly nominating his replacement. Powell's term as a governor runs until 2028.
body: The April meeting of the Fed’s monetary policy committee featured a lot of institutional news for a month in which the Fed kept monetary policy unchanged. The Fed continued its current pause for rate reductions at the conclusion of the April meeting of the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC), the central bank’s monetary policy decision making body. The Fed’s statement of current economic conditions and policy stance was relatively unchanged from March to April. The April meeting also likely marked the last FOMC decision featuring Chair Powell as Chair of the Fed’s Board of Governors after an eight-year run. President Trump’s choice for Chair, prior Federal Reserve Governor Kevin Warsh, appears set to be approved by the Senate and will likely chair the next meeting. However, in a change with precedent, while Powell will cease to be Chair of the Board when Warsh assumes leadership, Powell is retaining his seat as a Governor on the Board and thus a member of the FOMC.

# Powell says he will stay on Fed board after chairmanship ends, denying Trump a spot. Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell announced that he will remain on the Fed’s Board of Governors beyond the end of his term as chairman next month, breaking with tradition at the central bank and preventing President Donald Trump from quickly nominating his replacement. Powell, who was holding his last press conference as Fed chairman, previously said he would remain on the Fed board until the Justice Department investigation into him ended. On Wednesday, he confirmed that he plans to remain on the board. While his term as chairman ends on May 15, his term as a member of the board runs until 2028. “After my term as chair ends on May 15, I will continue to serve as a governor for a period of time to be determined.”. Powell’s plan to stay on the board will deprive Trump of having a majority of seats on the Fed’s Board of Governors.
